# Pnyx

Pnyx (pronounced "p-nix") is an open civic protocol for turning public problems into evidence-backed decisions, accountable action, and auditable public memory.

## Ethical root

We love humanity.

Technology serves society. Citizens retain judgment.

## Civic Loop

1. Raise the issue.
2. Assemble human, language-model, and hybrid skills in explicit roles.
3. Map claims, evidence, uncertainty, costs, rights, and capture risks.
4. Deliberate publicly.
5. Decide through human civic judgment and execute under accountable constraints.
6. Preserve outcomes as auditable public memory.

## Public website languages

- English: https://pnyx.dev/
- Greek: https://pnyx.dev/el/

## Canonical links

- Protocol and reference implementation: https://github.com/pikos-apikos/pnyx
- Website source: https://github.com/pikos-apikos/pnyx.dev

## Core constraints

- Political judgment is not delegated to a model.
- Disagreement, uncertainty, minority positions, and rejected warnings remain visible.
- The rules and failures of Pnyx pass through the same public loop.
- Publicly funded civic work returns to public memory.


## Public production journal

- One menu, generated everywhere: https://pnyx.dev/journal/generated-menu/
- Greek: https://pnyx.dev/el/journal/generated-menu/
- From the assembly to the horizon: https://pnyx.dev/journal/horizon/
- Greek: https://pnyx.dev/el/journal/horizon/
